key_quote: No theorem establishes that the product program's (un)satisfiability corresponds to the original programs' semantic (in)equivalence. The 15.6% accuracy means the tool cannot produce any verdict on the vast majority of inputs.
rationale: All reviewers identify the σ-bridge coercion framework as the primary conceptual advance. The blockers are execution gaps: missing soundness proof, 85% pipeline failure rate, overstated IR-erasure claims, and incomplete divergence class coverage. The core idea has recognized novelty; the implementation and formal rigor need major improvement.
